note: Current view coverage for the governed three-panel reinforced-miter caddy. Every candidate area is either assigned a zoom or records why another view would be redundant.
context_treatment: The sofa arm is existing context and can occlude the built work; arm-hidden variants expose the shell and joinery.

primary_views.shows: whole caddy over the modeled arm
clean show face and centered cup opening

candidate_areas.rationale: The full-thickness bore is a fabrication and fit feature whose wall is not clear in a whole-model view.
The most precise joint needs an arm-hidden close view showing both diagonal keys.
This corner is mirror-identical to the +X joint; a second zoom would duplicate the same operation.
The 1/4-inch nominal side clearance is fit-sensitive and too small to read in the primary views.
The simple flush bearing plane is already visible in the front and end views.
Plain crosscut ends are visible in the overview and need no separate operation zoom.
The solid renderer has no honest cutaway; the illustrated manual instead dimensions the compiled key axes and the process fact specifies glue in both holes.
